Application For Entry Of Default Judgment Against Defendant Buttner Corp.
Off Calendar Jul-28-2020 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: This matter came on calendar this day for Default Prove-Up Hearing as to defendant Buttner Corp.
Dept: 514. Judge: Gerardo C. Sandoval. Clerk: Kevin Dougherty. Reporter: Giselle Casey, CSR #8098. Telephone: 415-551-3680. Email: gcasey@sftc.org.

Jason M. Rose, Esq. appeared via CourtCall with plaintiff Mary Ann Trujillo, as Successor-in-Interest to, and as Wrongful Death Heir of, John Speck, Deceased, and also with plaintiffs James Speck and Robert Speck as Wrongful Death Heirs of John Speck, Deceased.
Ms. Trujillo and Messrs. James Speck and Robert Speck, all of whom appeared via CourtCall, were placed under oath but did not testify.
No appearance by defendant Buttner Corp.
Plaintiffs' Complaint for Survival/Wrongful Death was filed on September 24, 2013. Plaintiffs' First Amended Complaint was filed on January 22, 2014.
DOE Amendment to First Amended Complaint substituting defendant Buttner Corp. for DOE 317 was filed on July 23, 2014.
Substitute service of plaintiffs' First Amended Summons, First Amended Complaint and Statement of Damages occurred on August 15, 2014 and was effectuated on August 28, 2014.
Default as to defendant Buttner Corp. was entered on October 2, 2014.
Statements of Damages for all plaintiffs were filed on October 2, 2014.

After hearing the evidence presented, and good cause appearing, the Court rules in favor of plaintiffs Mary Ann Trujillo, as Successor-in-Interest to, and as Wrongful Death Heir of, John Speck, Deceased, and also with plaintiffs James Speck and Robert Speck as Wrongful Death Heirs of John Speck, Deceased, and against the defaulted defendant Buttner Corp., as follows:

Economic Damages - $466,531.88.
Non-Economic Damages - $3,000,000.00.
Total - $3,466,531.88.

Counsel for plaintiffs shall prepare the proposed Judgment and submit same to the Court for review and signature.

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Apr-14-2016 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Pamela E. Stevens of WFBM, LLP (415-781-7072), appearing for defendant / moving party D. Zelinsky & Sons, Inc.
Patrick R. Campbell of BRAYTON & PURCELL LLP (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiffs / opposing parties Mary Ann Trujillo, as successor-in-interest to and as wrongful death heirs of John Speck, deceased, et al.

RULING:
DEFENDANT D. ZELINSKY & SONS, INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT

Hearing held and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant D. Zelinsky & Sons, Inc.'s motion for summary judgment is denied. Defendant failed to sustain its initial burden of demonstrating that plaintiffs do not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that decedent James Speck was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to defendant. Plaintiffs' responses to defendant's special interrogatories were not factually devoid and, specifically, they identified Robert Cantley as a person with knowledge, identified what knowledge he has regarding Mr. Speck's exposure to asbestos-containing joint compound and taping mud by defendant's employees, and defendant failed to demonstrate that Mr. Cantley does not have such knowledge. Order signed in open court. Judge: Garrett L. Wong, Reporter: Erin F. Robinson of AIKEN WELCH, CSR # 12199 (Tel: 510-451-1580) (503/GLW)

Off Calendar Apr-07-2016 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Sara J. Savage of HUGO PARKER, LLP (415-808-0300), appearing for defendant / moving party Cahill Construction Co., Inc.
Simon S. Grille of BRAYTON & PURCELL LLP (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiffs / opposing parties Mary Ann Trujillo, as successor-in-interest to and as wrongful death heir of John Speck, deceased, et al.

RULING:
MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT OR IN THE ALTERNATIVE, MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION

Hearing held and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant Cahill Construction Co., Inc.'s motion for summary judgment is denied. Defendant failed to sustain its initial burden of demonstrating that plaintiffs do not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that Mr. Speck was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to defendant. Plaintiffs' responses to defendant's special interrogatories were not factually devoid and, specifically, they identified Robert Cantley as a person with knowledge and identified what knowledge he has and defendant failed to demonstrate that Mr. Cantley does not have such knowledge. Defendant also failed to demonstrate that plaintiffs stipulated that Mr. Cantley would not be offered as a witness against it. Additionally, were the court to get to plaintiffs' papers, Mr. Cantley's deposition testimony submitted by plaintiffs would be further grounds to conclude that defendant failed to sustain its initial burden. Defendant's alternate motion for summary adjudication of plaintiffs' claim for punitive damages is off calendar as moot as plaintiffs have dismissed such claim. Order signed in open court. Judge: Garrett L. Wong, Reporter: Tara L. Williams of AIKEN WELCH, CSR #13428 (Tel: 510-451-1580) (503/GLW)
